zh.lifeSummary: Jane Gitton於1455年出生。她和Robert Chandler De Oare至少有1個兒子。她去世於1475年享年20歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Jane Gitton于1455年出生。她和Robert Chandler De Oare至少有1个儿子。她去世于1475年享年20岁。

nameMeanings.name: Gitton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: 1 English: nickname from Middle English gitoun ‘military or guild banner’ (Anglo-Norman French guidon), though the post-medieval bearers may belong to (2).

variants.name: Gitting
meanings: Welsh, English: variant of   Gethin   or   Gitton  .  Compare  Gitten  .

variants.name: Gethin
meanings: Welsh: from the personal name Gethin, perhaps a soft-mutated form of cethin ‘dusky, swarthy’.

variants.name: Gethins
meanings: 1 Welsh, English, Scottish: (i) variant of Gethin , with English genitival or excrescent -s. (ii) possibly also a variant of Gitton (1) with post-medieval excrescent -s.
2 Irish: if not the Welsh name in Ireland, this name may have been adopted as a shortened form of Mag Eiteagáin, see McGettigan .

variants.name: Gitten
meanings: Welsh, English: variant of   Gethin   or   Gitton  .  Compare  Gitting  .

nameMeanings.name: Jane
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

variants.name: Jayne
meanings: Elaborated spelling of   Jane  , a form popularized no doubt by the American actress  Jayne Mansfield  (  Vera Jayne Palmer ,  1933–67 ) and British ice-dancer  Jayne Torvill  (  b. 1957 ).

variants.name: Jain
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

en.lifeSummary: Jane Gitton was born in 1455. She had at least 1 son with Robert Chandler De Oare. She died in 1475, at the age of 20.

fr.lifeSummary: Jane Gitton est née en 1455. Elle a eu au moins 1 fils avec Robert Chandler De Oare. Elle est décédée en 1475, à l’âge de 20 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Jane Gitton wurde 1455 geboren. Sie hatte mit Robert Chandler De Oare mindestens 1 Sohn. Sie starb 1475 im Alter von 20 Jahren.

it.lifeSummary: Jane Gitton è nata il nel 1455. Lei avuto almeno 1 figlio con Robert Chandler De Oare. Morì nel 1475, all'età di 20 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Jane Gittonは1455年に生まれた。彼女はRobert Chandler De Oareとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼女は1475年に20歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Jane Gitton(은)는 1455년 태어났다. 그녀는 Robert Chandler De Oare(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1475년 20세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Jane Gitton nasceu em 1455. Ela teve pelo menos 1 filho com Robert Chandler De Oare. Ela faleceu em 1475, com 20 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Jane Gitton родилась в 1455 г. У неё был по крайней мере 1 сын от Robert Chandler De Oare. Она умерла в 1475 г. в возрасте 20 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Jane Gitton nació en 1455. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo con Robert Chandler De Oare. Murió en 1475, a la edad de 20 años.
